This website separates content into three tracks: Notes, Blog, and Projects. Notes are quick personal understanding and can contain mistakes. Blog posts are formal publications with stronger validation. Projects pages document implementation details and architecture decisions. This separation keeps daily learning fast while preserving a reliable place for finalized ideas.
This page defines the difference between Notes and Blog content.
Goal: speed and utility Verification level: lightweight Status: may include incomplete or incorrect conclusions
Goal: publish reliable technical writing Verification level: stronger checking and revision Expected quality: references, traceable reasoning, clearer claims
If an idea is still evolving, keep it in Notes. If a topic is stable and can be defended with evidence, publish it as a Blog post.
This section is designed for quick capture and quick retrieval.
These notes represent personal understanding and may contain mistakes, omissions, or outdated details.
Use explicit status labels to communicate confidence: Draft: evolving notes, higher error risk Reviewed: rechecked and stabilized Archived: historical context, not primary guidance Open Status System
Machine Learning Deep Learning Computer Science
Start from category landing pages. Use sidebars for topic drill-down. Treat notes as working knowledge, not final authority.
Reviewed A category for practical and theoretical CS notes.
Algorithms and Data Structures Operating Systems Networks Databases
CS Foundations Process and Thread Model Status System

Draft This page is a placeholder for foundational topics such as: Time and space complexity Process and thread basics Memory hierarchy Network layers Note: This note may evolve and may contain errors while in progress.

Add concrete examples from real services and debugging cases.

Neural network fundamentals Optimization and training stability Model architecture patterns Experiment logs

Reviewed A category for models, optimization, evaluation, and practical implementation notes.
Fundamentals Deep Learning Training and Evaluation Experiments and Lessons Learned
ML Foundations Model Evaluation Basics Status System
Draft This note captures a minimal evaluation checklist: split strategy and leakage checks metric selection by task goal baseline comparison and error inspection
Connect this page with experiment templates and tracking conventions.

Draft This page is a placeholder for core concepts such as: Supervised vs unsupervised learning Bias-variance trade-off Overfitting and regularization Evaluation metrics Note: This note may evolve and may contain errors while in progress.
Use status labels to expose confidence and maturity.

Draft In-progress ideas May contain incorrect conclusions Prioritize speed over completeness Reviewed Rechecked with references or experiments Claims are clearer and more stable Suitable for repeated reuse Archived Kept for historical context May be outdated for current practice Not recommended as primary guidance
Start everything as Draft. Promote to Reviewed when confidence is sufficient. Mark Archived when superseded.
